new_classes.definition_en: Beverage produced by re-fermenting grape pomace (the solid remains of grapes after pressing) with water, classified under CN subheading 2206 00 10 (Chapter 22). Piquette is distinct from wine as it is not produced directly from fresh grapes or grape must, but from the fermentation of grape marc/pomace.

coverage_explanation: The legal text for CN 22060010 is brief: it identifies the commodity as "Piquette" and refers to Additional Note 9 to Chapter 22 for further definition. The new class Piquette is modelled as a subclass of FermentedBeverage (CN heading 2206), which correctly situates it in the hierarchy and distinguishes it from Wine (2204), Beer (2203), and other beverages. The codeString restriction pins the class to its exact 8-digit CN code. Coverage is not 1.0 because the full discriminating criteria depend on the content of Additional Note 9 to Chapter 22 (defining piquette as produced from grape marc/pomace re-fermented with water), which is referenced but not reproduced in the supplied legal text. Without the full text of Additional Note 9, the material-basis criterion (grape pomace/marc as raw material) can only be captured informally in the class definition rather than as a formal OWL restriction.
